mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth reflects the underlying momentum of economic output. Sustained increases in GDP signal rising corporate revenues and earnings, directly supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows negative correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year period, or the signal Rank IC is not statistically significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals the start of a labor market downturn, which reduces consumer income and spending, thereby lowering corporate earnings and driving S&P 500 prices down over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, the 60-day forward return of SPX is positive more than 50% of the time, indicating false negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising above 2.5%, the monetary tightening regime suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of CPIAUCSL above 3% and FEDFUNDS above 2.5% does not lead to negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing expansion regime defined by INDPRO exceeding 105, further increases in industrial production signal rising capacity utilization and economic strength, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 105, the subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, contradicting the hypothesized positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1.1 million units indicate a weakening housing sector, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and declining corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A drop in HOUST below 1100 fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) past 3.8% signal a very tight labor market, which forces the Fed to tighten monetary policy, thus reducing equity valuations over the 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE < 3.8% and PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k, the 60-day forward return of SPX is positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.5% constitutes a restrictive nominal stance, which increases the cost of capital and depresses equity prices, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicate robust consumer demand and disposable income growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS is rising month-over-month and UNRATE is falling, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases.
